history.content: Startup snapshot is healthy but visibility is partial: both task and schedule APIs are currently unavailable, and there’s one blocked item needing assistance (`cdb4a7b0...`) to finish the windows-shell-playbook branding/path/fallback update. Today’s notes emphasize that memory sync correctly no-op’d to avoid duplicate low-signal writes, intraday logs are intermittent (so no-file handling must stay graceful), and recent compaction/debug work clarified runtime directive behavior plus the X→Telegram hang root cause (`browser_send_to_telegram` unknown tool with an unfinished plan step). Where things left off: tighten fallback handling after tool failures, explicitly flag cross-signal status inconsistencies, and complete the blocked playbook update.
